title: Dental appliance using airway dialation for treating covid related breathing disorders
abstract: A molded breathing appliance for supporting a nasal oxygen tube disclosed having an upper bite rim, a lower bite rim, a moldable material disposed within the upper bite rim and lower bite rim for conforming to a patient's maxillary bite impression and mandibular bite impression, respectively, an L-shaped member extending outwardly perpendicular to the upper bite rim, and a transverse support disposed on the L-shaped member and configured with a plurality of sets of oxygen tube finger clamps to retain a nasal oxygen tube in proximity with a patient's nostrils.
claims:
Claims 6 I Claim: 1. A single piece, molded breathing appliance for supporting a nasal oxygen tube, comprising: an upper bite rim; a lower bite rim; an intermediary bridge connecting a portion of a lower surface of the upper bite rim to a portion of an upper surface of the lower bite rim; a moldable material disposed within the upper bite rim and lower bite rim, said moldable material adapted to set after conforming to a patientâ s maxillary bite impression and mandibular bite impression, respectively; an L- shaped member projecting outwardly and perpendicular to the upper bite rim, the L-shaped member comprising a flat horizontal spacer and flat vertical extension; and a transverse tube clamp disposed on the vertical extension of the L-shaped member and configured with a plurality of sets of opposed semi-cylindrical clamps adapted to cooperate to retain a nasal oxygen tube in proximity with a patientâs nostrils. 2. The breathing appliance of Claim 1 , wherein the semi-cylindrical clamps orient a flow of oxygen at an angle of between thirty and sixty degrees relative to the vertical extension. 3. The breathing appliance of Claim 1, wherein the semi-cylindrical clamps orient a flow of oxygen at an angle of approximately forty-five degrees relative to the vertical extension. 4. The breathing appliance of Claim 1 , wherein the moldable material sets after being heated to at least 140Â° F. 5. The breathing appliance of Claim 1 , wherein the moldable material includes EVA. 

 7 6. The breathing appliance of Claim 1 , wherein the lower bite rim is anteriorly offset from the upper bite rim to increase a patientâ s pharyngeal nasal breathing passage. 7. The breathing appliance of Claim 4, wherein the offset is between 1 and 2 mm. 8. A system for delivering oxygen to a patient through a nasal canula, comprising: a supply of oxygen; an oxygen tube; and a single piece, molded breathing appliance for supporting the oxygen tube, comprising an upper bite rim, a lower bite rim, an intermediary layer connecting a portion of a lower surface of the upper bite rim to a portion of an upper surface of the lower bite rim, a moldable material disposed within the upper bite rim and lower bite rim, said moldable material adapted to set after conforming to a patientâs maxillary bite impression and mandibular bite impression, respectively, an L-shaped member extending outwardly perpendicular to the upper bite rim, and a transverse support disposed on the L-shaped member and configured with a plurality of sets of oxygen tube finger clamps to retain the oxygen tube in proximity with a patientâs nostrils. 9. The system of Claim 8, wherein the moldable material sets after being heated to at least 140Â° F. 10. The system of Claim 8, wherein the moldable material includes EVA. 11. The system of Claim 8, wherein the lower bite rim is anteriorly offset from the upper bite rim to increase a patientâs pharyngeal nasal breathing passage. 12. The system of Claim 11, wherein the offset is between 1 and 2 mm. 13. A method for treating a COVID-related breathing disorder, comprising: 

 8 providing a dental appliance; conforming the dental appliance to a specific patientâs dental bite impression; attaching an oxygen tube to an extension attached to the dental appliance; and initiating a flow of oxygen through the oxygen tube.
